core_mechanism: domain-wise random symmetry breaking that seeds topologically stable defects at the junctions of independently-oriented domains, with the per-domain defect-formation probability fixed by the topology (homotopy) of the order-parameter space
brief_reason: The abstract reports defects (strings/disclinations) forming via bubble/domain formation across a symmetry-breaking phase transition, with the measured formation probability of 0.33 matching the 1/pi value predicted purely from the order-parameter space's topology S^2/Z_2.
